title: fix(ci): reset apidocs-sync branch to main + enable auto-merge
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
The `docs/apidocs-sync` branch was treated as long-lived and only fast-forwarded — but humans do edit `APIDOCS.md` directly on main (#10772, #10422, #10395, #10255, #10167, etc.), so the regen PR (#11008) ended up with a months-old merge-base and a conflict.

Changes:
- Reset `docs/apidocs-sync` to current `origin/main` each run, then force-push the regen on top. The branch carries no history worth preserving — the regen output is fully derived from the live spec.
- Enable auto-merge (`--squash`) on the PR so it lands once required checks pass instead of sitting open.

After this merges, the existing #11008 will likely need a one-time reset (or close + let the next deploy recreate it cleanly).

gist.headline: Self-healing docs
gist.blurb: Taught our API docs generator to automatically resolve its own merge conflicts and auto-merge when ready. Less manual clicking, more up-to-date docs.
gist.summary: Updated the API docs regeneration workflow to reset the docs/apidocs-sync branch to main before running and enabled auto-merge for the resulting PRs.
gist.impact: This prevents merge conflicts when humans manually edit APIDOCS.md and ensures automated spec updates land immediately without manual intervention.
gist.visual_concept: The Monitor Robot automatically organizing and stamping documents (API docs), while Polly the bee watches happily, representing automated CI/CD workflows.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head automatically stamping a neat stack of documents. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby wearing a tiny construction hard hat, giving a thumbs up. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
